summary: 最高法院在任期最后一天发布多项裁决，包括出生公民权、总统解雇独立机构官员权力等。特朗普政府试图通过行政命令终止出生公民权，但面临宪法挑战。同时，科罗拉多州初选中，民主社会主义者挑战现任议员。节目还讨论了联邦储备委员会独立性问题及委内瑞拉地震救援奇迹。

vocabulary.cn: Hak kewarganegaraan sejak lahir (hak untuk secara otomatis menjadi warga negara jika lahir di AS)
vocabulary.en: the right to citizenship for anyone born in a country's territory
vocabulary.excerpt: Expected among those rulings, a key one on birthright citizenship
vocabulary.example_cn: Diperkirakan di antara keputusan-keputusan ini, ada satu keputusan penting tentang hak kewarganegaraan sejak lahir.

vocabulary.cn: Perintah eksekutif (perintah yang dikeluarkan presiden yang memiliki kekuatan hukum)

vocabulary.example_cn: Presiden mencoba mengakhirinya dengan perintah eksekutif pada hari pertama menjabat.

vocabulary.cn: Surat suara melalui pos (surat suara yang dikirim melalui pos)

vocabulary.en: to cause something to be upside down or to disrupt completely
vocabulary.excerpt: the Fed has the power to upend global markets
vocabulary.example_cn: Federal Reserve memiliki kemampuan untuk menggulingkan pasar global.

vocabulary.cn: Perbedaan pendapat (khususnya pendapat berbeda dari hakim)

vocabulary.excerpt: Amy Coney Barrett noted in a dissent that's kind of bonkers
vocabulary.example_cn: Amy Coney Barrett menunjukkan dalam perbedaan pendapatnya bahwa ini agak gila.

vocabulary.en: fraud involving mortgage loans, such as falsifying information
vocabulary.excerpt: she was accused of some mortgage fraud and irregularities
vocabulary.example_cn: Dia dituduh melakukan penipuan kredit pemilikan rumah dan pelanggaran.

vocabulary.en: the act of ending something, especially employment
vocabulary.excerpt: her termination was related to that
vocabulary.example_cn: Pemecatannya terkait dengan hal ini.

vocabulary.en: claimed or alleged, often without proof

vocabulary.example_cn: 她据称被解雇。

vocabulary.en: to be in agreement with; to match
vocabulary.excerpt: she doesn't comport with the agency's priorities
vocabulary.example_cn: 她不符合该机构的优先事项。

vocabulary.en: extensive power or control
vocabulary.excerpt: presidents now have vast authority to remove anybody for any reason
vocabulary.example_cn: 总统现在拥有巨大权力，可以以任何理由解雇任何人。

vocabulary.cn: 现实检验；提醒面对现实
vocabulary.en: something that reminds people of the true nature of a situation
vocabulary.excerpt: there are some reality checks to that power
vocabulary.example_cn: 这种权力有一些现实制约。

vocabulary.en: an attitude of doubt or disbelief
vocabulary.excerpt: who expressed skepticism
vocabulary.example_cn: Siapa yang menyatakan keraguan.

vocabulary.cn: Amendemen Keempat Belas (Konstitusi AS, mengatur kewarganegaraan berdasarkan kelahiran, dll.)
vocabulary.en: the constitutional amendment granting citizenship to all persons born in the U.S.
vocabulary.excerpt: the Constitution, 14th Amendment, says that all citizens born in the United States

vocabulary.example_cn: Semua orang yang lahir di AS dan tunduk pada yurisdiksinya adalah warga negara.

vocabulary.example_cn: Ini adalah pandangan pinggiran dalam hukum AS.

vocabulary.excerpt: Democratic Socialist Looks to Unseat Incumbent in Colorado Primary

vocabulary.excerpt: Unseat Incumbent in Colorado Primary
vocabulary.example_cn: 在科罗拉多州初选中击败现任议员。

vocabulary.en: something that puts someone at a disadvantage
vocabulary.excerpt: Will her past tweets praising communism be a liability?
vocabulary.example_cn: 她过去赞扬共产主义的推文会成为不利因素吗？

sentences.en: The Supreme Court has said, no, we will not have that independent check. You will not be watchdogs anymore. You will be lapdogs.
sentences.cn: 最高法院已经说了，不，我们不会有那种独立的制衡。你们不再是看门狗了。你们将是哈巴狗。
sentences.structure: 复合句：主句+直接引语（三个并列句）
sentences.analysis: watchdogs和lapdogs是比喻，watchdogs指监督者，lapdogs指顺从者。直接引语增强语气。

sentences.en: I'm a nobody. I'm just a young man who spent five days trapped beneath a building.
sentences.cn: 我是个无名小卒。我只是一个被困在建筑物下五天的年轻人。
sentences.structure: 两个简单句，第二句含定语从句修饰young man
sentences.analysis: nobody口语化，意为无名小卒；trapped beneath a building是过去分词短语作定语。

sentences.en: I do regret my tweets. And that's something that I think has brought a lot of division, and that's something I regret.
sentences.cn: 我确实后悔我的推文。那是我认为带来了很多分裂的事情，也是我后悔的事情。
sentences.structure: 并列句，第二句含两个定语从句修饰something
sentences.analysis: do强调；that's something that...是强调结构；division指分裂。

sentences.en: The president said it himself. It is the largest expansion of executive power in over 100 years, and that is exactly why we should be worried about it.
sentences.cn: 总统自己说的。这是100多年来行政权力的最大扩张，而这正是我们应该担心的原因。
sentences.structure: 并列句，第二句含表语从句和why引导的定语从句
sentences.analysis: expansion of executive power指行政权力扩张；exactly why强调原因。

sentences.en: Expected among those rulings, a key one on birthright citizenship, something the president tried to end with an executive order on his first day in office last year.
sentences.cn: 在这些裁决中，预计会有一项关于出生公民权的关键裁决，这是总统去年上任第一天试图通过行政命令终止的事情。
sentences.structure: 省略句：完整为A key one on birthright citizenship is expected among those rulings, something...是同位语
sentences.analysis: birthright citizenship出生公民权；executive order行政命令；on his first day in office上任第一天。

sentences.en: I think it's very bad for our nation. I don't know. It's up to them. But in terms of for the good of the country, it would be great if they did the -- they didn't allow it.
sentences.cn: 我认为这对我们的国家非常不利。我不知道。这取决于他们。但就国家利益而言，如果他们做了 --  -- 他们不允许的话，那就太好了。
sentences.structure: 并列复合句，含if引导的条件状语从句
sentences.analysis: in terms of就...而言；for the good of the country为了国家利益；did the -- they didn't allow it是口误修正。

sentences.en: And they were able -- the court was able to treat the Federal Reserve different from other agencies for a host of different reasons.
sentences.cn: 而且他们能够 --  -- 法院能够基于一系列不同的原因，将美联储与其他机构区别对待。
sentences.structure: 简单句，插入语the court was able to，for引导原因状语
sentences.analysis: a host of许多；treat...different from区别对待。

sentences.en: Something that doesn't get talked a lot about is this idea that the Fed is funded differently than other entities.
sentences.cn: 一个不常被提及的事情是，美联储的融资方式与其他实体不同。
sentences.structure: 主句含定语从句修饰something，表语从句含that同位语从句
sentences.analysis: doesn't get talked a lot about被动语态；funded differently融资方式不同。

sentences.en: It's funded, in large part, on account of securities that it holds.
sentences.cn: 它在很大程度上依靠其持有的证券来融资。
sentences.structure: 简单句，插入语in large part，on account of引导原因状语，that定语从句修饰securities
sentences.analysis: in large part在很大程度上；on account of由于；securities证券。

sentences.en: So, they could treat it as not like a typical agency, like the kind that are funded by Congress, and they can carve it out and say that, because of the Fed's unique stature and -- and also, as a policy matter, because the Fed has the power to upend global markets, we have to keep it independent.
sentences.cn: 所以，他们可以将其视为不像典型的机构，比如那种由国会资助的机构，他们可以将其单独划分出来，并说，由于美联储独特的地位 --  -- 而且，作为一个政策问题，因为美联储有能力颠覆全球市场，我们必须保持其独立性。
sentences.structure: 并列复合句，含多个状语从句和宾语从句
sentences.analysis: carve it out划分出来；unique stature独特地位；as a policy matter作为政策问题；upend global markets颠覆全球市场。

sentences.en: Now, Amy Coney Barrett noted in a dissent that's kind of bonkers, because they're sort of being a little cute with treating some agencies one way and some another.
sentences.cn: 现在，艾米·科尼·巴雷特在异议中指出这有点疯狂，因为他们对待一些机构是一种方式，对待另一些又是另一种方式，有点耍小聪明。
sentences.structure: 复合句，主句+宾语从句，含because原因状语从句
sentences.analysis: dissent异议；bonkers疯狂的；being a little cute耍小聪明；treating...one way and...another区别对待。

sentences.en: It's a little different with Rebecca Slaughter over at the Federal Trade Commission, because that was, Can we just fire because we don't want her? Because she doesn't comport with the agency's priorities, with the president's priorities?
sentences.cn: 联邦贸易委员会的丽贝卡·斯劳特的情况有点不同，因为那是，我们能仅仅因为不想要她就解雇她吗？因为她不符合机构的优先事项，不符合总统的优先事项？
sentences.structure: 复合句，主句+because原因状语从句，含直接引语疑问句
sentences.analysis: comport with符合；priorities优先事项。

sentences.en: The Supreme Court yesterday said that's fine.
sentences.cn: 最高法院昨天说那没问题。
sentences.structure: 简单句，含宾语从句
sentences.analysis: that's fine口语化，表示认可。

sentences.en: Just the president's ability to fire somebody who disagrees with his --
sentences.cn: 只是总统解雇不同意他的人的权力 --  --
sentences.structure: 名词短语，含定语从句修饰somebody
sentences.analysis: ability to fire解雇权；disagrees with his后面省略，指不同意他的观点。

sentences.en: We can fire people that are in independent agencies -- if we're in the executive branch, except at the Fed, because they've got some special powers.
sentences.cn: 我们可以解雇独立机构里的人 --  -- 如果我们是在行政部门，除了美联储，因为他们有一些特殊权力。
sentences.structure: 复合句，主句+if条件状语从句+except介词短语+because原因状语从句
sentences.analysis: independent agencies独立机构；executive branch行政部门；special powers特殊权力。

sentences.en: And what stops the president from firing them the next day for -- for whatever reason that that president so chose?
sentences.cn: 又有什么能阻止总统第二天以 --  -- 以总统选择的任何理由解雇他们呢？
sentences.structure: 特殊疑问句，含定语从句修饰reason
sentences.analysis: stop...from doing阻止；for whatever reason无论什么理由；so chose如此选择。

sentences.en: According to what the Supreme Court ruled yesterday, presidents now have vast authority to remove anybody for any reason that they want.
sentences.cn: 根据最高法院昨天的裁决，总统现在拥有广泛的权力，可以以他们想要的任何理由罢免任何人。
sentences.structure: 复合句，according to引导状语，主句含定语从句修饰reason
sentences.analysis: vast authority广泛权力；remove罢免；for any reason they want以任何他们想要的理由。

sentences.en: So big picture, the Constitution, 14th Amendment, says that all citizens born in the United States and subject to the jurisdiction thereof, are citizens of the United States.
sentences.cn: 所以从宏观上看，宪法第十四修正案规定，所有在美国出生并受其管辖的公民，都是美国公民。
sentences.structure: 复合句，主句+宾语从句，从句含过去分词短语和形容词短语作定语
sentences.analysis: big picture宏观上；14th Amendment第十四修正案；subject to the jurisdiction thereof受其管辖。

sentences.en: And moreover, that line, "subject to the jurisdiction thereof," means that it's -- it's a little bit squishier.
sentences.cn: 而且，那句"受其管辖"意味着它 --  -- 它有点模糊。
sentences.structure: 复合句，主句+宾语从句
sentences.analysis: moreover而且；squishier更模糊，口语化。

sentences.en: And as we saw at the oral argument in this case, whether it's eight of the justices or seven of the justices, a lot of them are just not sympathetic to the idea.
sentences.cn: 正如我们在本案的口头辩论中看到的，无论是八位大法官还是七位大法官，他们中的很多人就是不同情这个观点。
sentences.structure: 复合句，as引导方式状语从句，主句含whether...or...结构
sentences.analysis: oral argument口头辩论；sympathetic to同情，支持。

topics.title: 美国最高法院与出生公民权
topics.content: 美国宪法第十四修正案规定，凡在美国出生并受其管辖者即为美国公民。特朗普政府试图通过行政命令终止出生公民权，但最高法院在口头辩论中多数大法官对此表示怀疑，认为宪法文本明确，政府面临 uphill battle。
topics.keywords: birthright citizenship · 14th Amendment · executive order · uphill battle

topics.title: 总统解雇独立机构官员的权力
topics.content: Mahkamah Agung memutuskan bahwa presiden berhak memberhentikan pejabat lembaga independen, namun Federal Reserve diperlakukan berbeda karena posisinya yang unik (misalnya, pendanaan sendiri, mempengaruhi pasar global). Putusan ini memperluas kewenangan besar presiden, tetapi Amy Coney Barrett dalam dissent-nya mengkritik perbedaan ini sebagai tidak konsisten.
topics.keywords: vast authority · Federal Reserve · dissent · independent agency

topics.title: Pemilihan Pendahuluan Colorado dan Sosialis Demokrat
topics.content: Dalam pemilihan pendahuluan Colorado, seorang sosialis demokrat berusaha menggeser petahana, mencerminkan kebangkitan sayap kiri di Partai Demokrat. Cuitan kandidat yang memuji komunisme di masa lalu dapat menjadi beban, memicu diskusi tentang arah masa depan partai.

topics.title: Keajaiban Penyelamatan Gempa Venezuela
topics.content: Setelah gempa bumi dahsyat di Venezuela, tim penyelamat berhasil menyelamatkan korban selamat dari reruntuhan, termasuk hewan peliharaan. Seorang pria muda yang terperangkap selama lima hari setelah diselamatkan mengatakan dirinya hanyalah seorang biasa, menyoroti keajaiban hidup di tengah bencana.

quiz.question: Berdasarkan teks, apa yang dimaksud dengan 'birthright citizenship'?
options: A. Kewarganegaraan yang diperoleh melalui naturalisasi
B. Kewarganegaraan yang diperoleh secara otomatis karena lahir di AS
C. Kewarganegaraan yang diperoleh melalui garis keturunan orang tua
D. Kewarganegaraan yang diperoleh melalui pernikahan
quiz.explanation: Teks menyebutkan 'birthright citizenship' terkait dengan Amandemen Keempat Belas Konstitusi, yaitu kewarganegaraan otomatis bagi mereka yang lahir di AS. Opsi B benar.

quiz.question: Apa arti 'uphill battle' dalam teks?
quiz.explanation: 'Uphill battle' adalah ungkapan tetap yang berarti perjuangan yang sulit. Teks mengatakan pemerintah menghadapi uphill battle dalam kasus ini, yaitu penuh kesulitan.

quiz.question: Berdasarkan konteks, apa yang dimaksud dengan 'dissent' dalam konteks Mahkamah Agung?
quiz.explanation: Di Mahkamah Agung, 'dissent' merujuk pada pendapat berbeda yang dikeluarkan hakim yang tidak setuju dengan pendapat mayoritas. Dalam teks, Amy Coney Barrett dalam dissent-nya mengkritik pendapat mayoritas.

quiz.question: Apa perbedaan keputusan Mahkamah Agung tentang pemecatan Federal Reserve dengan lembaga independen lainnya?
options: A. Pejabat Federal Reserve dapat dipecat sewaktu-waktu
B. Federal Reserve dilindungi karena statusnya yang unik
C. Semua lembaga independen tunduk pada aturan yang sama
D. Federal Reserve tidak tunduk pada yurisdiksi Mahkamah Agung
quiz.explanation: Teks asli menyatakan bahwa pengadilan membedakan Federal Reserve dari lembaga lain karena pendanaan uniknya dan kemampuannya memengaruhi pasar global, sehingga memungkinkannya tetap independen.

quiz.question: Apa dasar hukum utama pemerintahan Trump tentang kewarganegaraan berdasarkan kelahiran?
options: A. Amandemen Keempat Belas mendukung secara jelas
quiz.explanation: Teks asli menyebutkan bahwa pemerintahan Trump menganggap frasa 'subject to the jurisdiction thereof' dalam Amandemen Keempat Belas ambigu, sehingga berusaha membatasi kewarganegaraan berdasarkan kelahiran.

quiz.question: Apa kontroversi utama yang dihadapi kandidat 'Democratic Socialist' yang disebutkan dalam teks?
options: B. Tweet masa lalu yang memuji komunisme
C. Debat dengan anggota parlemen saat ini
quiz.explanation: Teks asli bertanya langsung: 'Will her past tweets praising communism be a liability?' menunjukkan bahwa tweet masa lalunya adalah kontroversi utama.

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Anchor
paragraphs.text: -- Indianapolis International Airport. This was just one of two grenades inside a passenger's checked bag. The passenger was immediately questioned by officials. They haven't said yet if there will be charges. We will continue to track any developments there. That does it for CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Brad Smith. CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: -- 印第安纳波利斯国际机场。这只是乘客托运行李中的两枚手榴弹之一。该乘客立即被官员询问。他们尚未说明是否会提出指控。我们将继续追踪任何进展。CNN头条快讯到此结束。我是布拉德·史密斯。奥黛·科尼什主持的《CNN今晨》现在开始。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Today in the group chat: the Supreme Court. Will it give Donald Trump more presidential power? And will today's ruling on birthright citizenship be another win for the executive branch?
paragraphs.cn: 今天群聊话题：最高法院。它会赋予特朗普更多总统权力吗？今天关于出生公民权的裁决会是行政部门的又一次胜利吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: The Supreme Court has said, no, we will not have that independent check. You will not be watchdogs anymore. You will be lapdogs.
paragraphs.cn: 最高法院已经表示，不，我们不会允许那种独立制衡。你们不再是监督者。你们将成为哈巴狗。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Is the Democratic Party tipping to the extreme? What a Michigan Senate candidate says about the party's future.
paragraphs.cn: 民主党是否在走向极端？一位密歇根州参议员候选人对该党未来的看法。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: I'm a nobody. I'm just a young man who spent five days trapped beneath a building.
paragraphs.cn: 我是个无名小卒。我只是一个在建筑物下被困五天的年轻人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Miracles emerge from the devastation in Venezuela. People, even pets being rescued from rubble. DARIALIZA AVILA CHEVALIER (D),
paragraphs.cn: 奇迹从委内瑞拉的废墟中涌现。人们，甚至宠物从瓦砾中被救出。达里亚莉萨·阿维拉·切瓦利埃（民主党），

paragraphs.speaker: New York Congressional Candidate
paragraphs.text: I do regret my tweets. And that's something that I think has brought a lot of division, and that's something I regret.
paragraphs.cn: 我确实后悔我的推文。我认为这带来了很多分裂，这是我后悔的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: A rising star on the left answering for her old social media post. Will her past tweets praising communism be a liability?
paragraphs.cn: 一位左翼新星为她过去的社交媒体帖子辩护。她过去赞扬共产主义的推文会成为负担吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: The president said it himself. It is the largest expansion of executive power in over 100 years, and that is exactly why we should be worried about it.
paragraphs.cn: 总统自己说过。这是100多年来行政权力最大的一次扩张，这正是我们应该担心的原因。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The Supreme Court enters the final day of its session today after already giving Donald Trump several key victories and some setbacks. So, where will these final cases fall? Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish, and it's just a few hours before the Supreme Court will publish their final decisions of this term and then head for summer recess. Expected among those rulings, a key one on birthright citizenship, something the president tried to end with an executive order on his first day in office last year.
paragraphs.cn: 最高法院今天进入会期最后一天，此前已给予唐纳德·特朗普几次关键胜利和一些挫折。那么，这些最终案件将如何裁决？大家早上好。我是奥黛·科尼什，距离最高法院公布本任期最终裁决并进入夏季休会还有几个小时。预计在这些裁决中，有一项关于出生公民权的关键裁决，这是总统去年上任第一天试图通过行政命令终止的。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Birthright citizenship. What are your thoughts? And will you accept it if it rules against you? DONALD TRUMP, U.S. PRESIDENT; Well, I guess I have to accept. It's the Supreme Court, so I'll accept. I think it's very bad for our nation. I don't know. It's up to them. But in terms of for the good of the country, it would be great if they did the -- they didn't allow it.
paragraphs.cn: 出生公民权。你怎么看？如果裁决对你不利，你会接受吗？美国总统唐纳德·特朗普：嗯，我想我必须接受。这是最高法院，所以我会接受。我认为这对我们的国家非常不利。我不知道。这取决于他们。但就国家利益而言，如果他们不允许这样做，那将非常好。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Joining me now in the group chat, Francesca Chambers, White House correspondent for USA Today; Elliot Williams, CNN legal analyst and former federal prosecutor; Chuck Rocha, Democratic strategist and former senior adviser to the Bernie Sanders presidential campaigns; and Ashley Davis, former White House official under President George W. Bush. You guys, thank you so much for being here. Starting with something easy. You know, the Supreme Court.
paragraphs.cn: 现在加入我们群聊的是《今日美国》白宫记者弗朗西斯卡·钱伯斯、CNN法律分析师兼前联邦检察官埃利奥特·威廉姆斯、民主党策略师兼伯尼·桑德斯总统竞选活动前高级顾问查克·罗查，以及乔治·W·布什总统时期的前白宫官员阿什莉·戴维斯。各位，非常感谢你们的到来。从简单的问题开始。你知道，最高法院。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And it's been kind of a weird week for the White House, right? Like, something like mail-in ballots --
paragraphs.cn: 对白宫来说，这周有点奇怪，对吧？比如，像邮寄选票这样的事情 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- didn't quite work out. And I want to come to birthright citizenship. But first, can you talk to me about the -- the rejection of the attempt to fire Lisa Cook?
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 并没有完全成功。我想谈谈出生公民权。但首先，你能跟我谈谈关于解雇丽莎·库克的尝试被驳回的事情吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Because this was a weird one.
paragraphs.cn: 因为这很奇怪。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I'm not quite sure what happened in the end.
paragraphs.cn: 我不太确定最后发生了什么。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: I'll tell you exactly why. And they were able -- the court was able to treat the Federal Reserve different from other agencies for a host of different reasons. Something that doesn't get talked a lot about is this idea that the Fed is funded differently than other entities. It's funded, in large part, on account of securities that it holds.
paragraphs.cn: 我来告诉你确切原因。法院能够出于一系列不同的原因，将美联储与其他机构区别对待。一个不常被讨论的问题是，美联储的资金来源与其他实体不同。它的资金主要来自其持有的证券。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Right? So, they could treat it as not like a typical agency, like the kind that are funded by Congress, and they can carve it out and say that, because of the Fed's unique stature and -- and also, as a policy matter, because the Fed has the power to upend global markets, we have to keep it independent. Now, Amy Coney Barrett noted in a dissent that's kind of bonkers, because they're sort of being a little cute with treating some agencies one way and some another.
paragraphs.cn: 对吧？所以，他们可以不把它当作典型的机构，比如那些由国会资助的机构，他们可以将其单独划分出来，并说，由于美联储独特的地位，而且作为政策问题，因为美联储有能力颠覆全球市场，我们必须保持其独立性。现在，艾米·科尼·巴雷特在异议中指出这有点疯狂，因为他们对待一些机构是一种方式，对待另一些又是另一种方式，这有点耍小聪明。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Now, in terms of the specifics of Lisa Cook, she was accused of some --
paragraphs.cn: 现在，就丽莎·库克的具体情况而言，她被指控 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Mortgage fraud, yes.
paragraphs.cn: 抵押贷款欺诈，是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: -- mortgage fraud and irregularities and was -- like, her termination was related to that. Right? Or at least her purported termination. It's a little different with Rebecca Slaughter over at the Federal Trade Commission, because that was, Can we just fire because we don't want her? Because she doesn't comport with the agency's priorities, with the president's priorities? Pardon me. The Supreme Court yesterday said that's fine.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 抵押贷款欺诈和违规行为，她的解雇与此有关，对吧？或者至少是她声称的解雇。联邦贸易委员会的丽贝卡·斯劳特的情况有点不同，因为问题是：我们能不能仅仅因为不想要她就解雇她？因为她不符合该机构的优先事项，不符合总统的优先事项？抱歉。最高法院昨天说，这没问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Williams
paragraphs.text: Just the president's ability to fire somebody who disagrees with his -- (CROSSTALK)
paragraphs.cn: 只是总统解雇不同意他观点的人的能力 --  -- （交叉对话）

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: We can fire people that are in independent agencies --
paragraphs.cn: 我们可以解雇独立机构的人 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- if we're in the executive branch, except at the Fed, because they've got some special powers.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 如果我们在行政部门，除了美联储，因为他们有一些特殊权力。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: They've got special juice.
paragraphs.cn: 他们有特殊的影响力。

paragraphs.speaker: Ashley Davis
paragraphs.text: My question is, though --
paragraphs.cn: 但我的问题是 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: -- for that, I think that you still have to nominate a Democrat within the party. It still has to be a Democrat nominee. So, her slot wouldn't go to a Republican nominee. It has to still go through Schumer.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 对此，我认为你仍然需要提名党内的一名民主党人。仍然必须是民主党提名人。所以，她的职位不会给共和党提名人。仍然需要经过舒默。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Well, that's what the law says, but who knows what --
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，法律是这么说的，但谁知道 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: I mean, so, think about it this way. Yes, technically, a Democratic nominee could be put in the FTC or FTC or whatever else. And what stops the president from firing them the next day for -- for whatever reason that that president so chose? According to what the Supreme Court ruled yesterday, presidents now --
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，这样想吧。是的，从技术上讲，民主党提名人可以被任命到FTC或其他机构。但什么能阻止总统第二天就解雇他们 --  -- 出于总统选择的任何理由？根据最高法院昨天的裁决，总统现在 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Has the authority, yes.
paragraphs.cn: 拥有这个权力，是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: -- have vast authority to remove anybody for any reason that they want.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 拥有广泛的权力，可以以任何理由随意解雇任何人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. So, there are some reality checks to that power.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。那么，这种权力有一些现实检验。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: People think maybe the birthright citizenship ruling will be one of those reality checks.
paragraphs.cn: 人们认为，或许出生公民权的裁决将成为这些现实检验之一。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Why was this considered a long shot? And I know we've heard the arguments since, so I don't know if there are particular justices you noticed, who expressed skepticism.
paragraphs.cn: 为什么这被认为希望渺茫？我知道我们之后听到了各种论点，所以我不确定你是否注意到有哪位大法官表达了怀疑。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Right, right. So big picture, the Constitution, 14th Amendment, says that all citizens born in the United States and subject to the jurisdiction thereof, are citizens of the United States. That has been a --
paragraphs.cn: 对，对。那么从大局来看，宪法第十四修正案规定，所有在美国出生并受其管辖的人都是美国公民。这一直是 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And the Trump administration has taken a line we've heard in conservative circles for a long time, which is, hey, that was just an amendment for --
paragraphs.cn: 而特朗普政府采取了一种我们在保守派圈子中早已听过的说法，即，嘿，那只是针对 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- the American descendants of slavery.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 美国奴隶的后代。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And it didn't really mean much --
paragraphs.cn: 而且它实际上并不意味着 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- for everyone else. So. what is happening? Why --
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 对其他人有什么意义。那么，发生了什么？为什么 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: And moreover, that line, "subject to the jurisdiction thereof," means that it's -- it's a little bit squishier. Now, that is a fringe view in American law. Let's be clear. It's not a widely held view in the legal mainstream. And as we saw at the oral argument in this case, whether it's eight of the justices or seven of the justices, a lot of them are just not sympathetic to the idea.
paragraphs.cn: 此外，"受其管辖"这一表述意味着它有点模糊。现在，这是美国法律中的边缘观点。需要明确的是，这在法律主流中并非广泛持有的观点。正如我们在本案的口头辩论中所见，无论是八位大法官还是七位大法官，其中很多人都对这个想法不以为然。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Oh, that's a big number.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，那是个大数字。

paragraphs.speaker: I -- I -- Cornish
paragraphs.text: We're not looking at, like, one or two.
paragraphs.cn: 我们说的可不是一两个。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Hard to say and forget, you know -- and Lord strike me down for making a prediction that -- that's a little kooky. But it's just -- just looking at how the oral argument went. You had John Roberts, the chief justice, saying right there in court, Look, the world has changed, but the Constitution has not. Or I think it was same world, different Constitution.
paragraphs.cn: 很难说又很难忘，你知道 --  -- 愿主惩罚我做出这样一个有点古怪的预测。但只是看看口头辩论的情况。首席大法官约翰·罗伯茨在法庭上直接说，看，世界变了，但宪法没变。或者我认为是同一个世界，不同的宪法。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: He is saying that, even though the country might have a different attitude toward immigration today than it did in 1789, the simple fact is, the Constitution is plainly clear on its face, and the administration just has an uphill battle on this one. So, I think they lose. It's just hard to see how much. FRANCESCA CHAMBERS, WHITE HOUSE CORRESPONDENT, "USA TODAY": Jumping in as the resident White House correspondent on the panel, I think that President Donald Trump knows that.
paragraphs.cn: 他是在说，尽管如今国家对移民的态度可能与1789年不同，但简单的事实是，宪法字面意思很清楚，政府在这件事上处境艰难。所以，我认为他们会输。只是很难看出输多少。《今日美国》白宫记者弗朗西斯卡·钱伯斯：作为小组中的常驻白宫记者，我认为唐纳德·特朗普总统知道这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: You have heard him for months now, attacking justices on the Supreme Court by name, who he thinks are going to potentially rule against him on birthright citizenship; tearing them down over other rulings they've made that have been directly related to tariffs and other issues. But the way that he's been raising birthright citizenship for months in this context suggests that the administration knows, like you said, that this was -- this was a long shot.
paragraphs.cn: 几个月来，你听到他点名攻击最高法院的法官，他认为这些人可能会在出生公民权问题上对他不利；他贬低他们在关税和其他问题上做出的裁决。但他几个月来一直这样提起出生公民权，表明政府知道，就像你说的，这希望渺茫。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: One other thing. Chuck, I want to ask you about the mail-in voting and the voting fight, because in the end, the court seemed to say, look, if things are postmarked by the day, it's fine. But this is still a major sort of talking point on the right. Does it feel like it is something that is helpful for Democrats going forward; that they're not fighting redistricting, mail-in ballot, like, multiple battles?
paragraphs.cn: 还有一件事。查克，我想问你关于邮寄投票和投票斗争的问题，因为最终，法院似乎说，如果东西在当天盖上邮戳，那就没问题。但这仍然是右翼的一个主要话题。这是否感觉对民主党未来有帮助？他们不用同时应对重新划分选区、邮寄选票等多场战斗？

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: I think when you're talking about letting people legally vote, it's a good thing. And I think one of the message points we will have when there was a group text last night talking about this, a lot of these ballots are military ballots. People serving overseas, people that are on ships. This is a big part of why we even created this law --
paragraphs.cn: 我认为当你在谈论让人们合法投票时，这是一件好事。我认为昨晚我们讨论这个问题的群聊中有一个信息点，这些选票很多是军人的选票。在海外服役的人，在船上的人。这是我们制定这项法律的重要原因 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: -- is that when you're overseas and you're military, you should be able to vote in your home district where your house resides. And I think that's a winning message every day when you're letting Americans, especially service members, do your democratic -- democratic duty.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- Ketika Anda bertugas di luar negeri, Anda harus bisa memilih di distrik tempat tinggal Anda. Saya pikir ini adalah pesan yang bisa menang setiap hari, ketika Anda membuat orang Amerika, terutama militer, menjalankan tugas demokrasi mereka.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Ashley, is there a ruling that you've already seen that you were surprised by, or one you're hoping to hear about today?
paragraphs.cn: Ashley, apakah ada keputusan yang sudah Anda lihat dan mengejutkan Anda, atau keputusan yang Anda harapkan hari ini?

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: No, I think the transgender athlete issue today will be interesting. I think, on the opposite side, most people think he'll lose the birthright. Most people think he'll win that that the birthright -- the birth. I'm sorry, the transgender.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, saya pikir masalah tentang atlet transgender hari ini akan menarik. Saya pikir, di sisi lain, kebanyakan orang berpikir dia akan kalah dalam hak kewarganegaraan sejak lahir. Kebanyakan orang berpikir dia akan menang dalam hak -- -- lahir. Maaf, transgender.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: State bans on transgender athletes in youth sports.
paragraphs.cn: Negara bagian melarang atlet transgender berpartisipasi dalam olahraga remaja.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Exactly. What's interesting to me were the dissents yesterday. So, you have, like, Roberts, who dissented at least twice. I mean, in regards to the two FTC and the Reserve. And then Brett Kavanaugh --
paragraphs.cn: Benar. Yang menarik perhatian saya adalah pendapat dissenting kemarin. Misalnya, Roberts setidaknya dua kali mengajukan dissenting. Maksud saya, dua kasus tentang Federal Trade Commission dan Reserve Board. Lalu Brett Kavanaugh -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: -- actually was one of the dissents for -- he sided with Roberts and the minority.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- sebenarnya salah satu yang dissenting -- -- dia berada di pihak Roberts dan minoritas.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: And then Amy Coney Barrett, who does a lot, do the --
paragraphs.cn: Lalu Amy Coney Barrett, dia melakukan banyak -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And who I've already seen as a kind of target in sort of right-wing media circles.
paragraphs.cn: Dan saya sudah melihatnya menjadi semacam target di kalangan media sayap kanan.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Yes. I think a couple things. Just in American history, always read the dissents, because that's where you're seeing sort of what the future of American law is.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Saya pikir ada beberapa poin. Dalam sejarah Amerika, selalu penting membaca pendapat dissenting, karena dari situ Anda bisa melihat ke mana arah hukum Amerika di masa depan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Just ask Clarence Thomas.
paragraphs.cn: Benar. Tanyakan saja pada Clarence Thomas.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Ask Clarence Thomas. And where they're disagreeing. But also, the big fight yesterday was Amy Coney Barrett versus Justice Alito over terminations and firings. And there was just -- you're watching fights among the conservatives, which are really fascinating.
paragraphs.cn: Tanyakan pada Clarence Thomas. Lihat di mana mereka berbeda pendapat. Dan, pertarungan besar kemarin adalah Amy Coney Barrett vs Hakim Alito tentang pemecatan dan pemberhentian. Yang Anda lihat adalah pertarungan antara konservatif, yang sangat menarik.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. The seeds of discontent become law at some point.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Benih ketidakpuasan pada suatu saat akan menjadi hukum.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: We were reading her dissent that's, like, 80 billion pages that happened yesterday, top.
paragraphs.cn: Kami membaca pendapat dissentingnya yang sepanjang 800 miliar halaman kemarin, benar-benar panjang lebar.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Hey, you guys wanted on camera, too. So, we'll see. All right, you guys, stay with me. We've got a lot to talk about this hour coming up on CNN THIS MORNING. Today is primary day in Colorado. There's a Gen Z congressional candidate taking on an incumbent who has elected -- who was elected the year before she was born. So, are we looking at a generational shift? Plus, a World Cup knockout. Paraguay stuns Germany, defeating the four-time world champions.
paragraphs.cn: Hei, kalian juga ingin tampil di kamera. Kita lihat saja. Oke, semuanya, ikuti saya. Banyak topik yang akan dibahas di CNN This Morning hari ini. Hari ini adalah hari pemilihan pendahuluan di Colorado. Seorang kandidat Kongres Generasi Z menantang petahana yang sudah menjabat sebelum dia lahir. Jadi, apakah kita menyaksikan pergantian generasi? Selain itu, kejutan Piala Dunia: Paraguay mengalahkan Jerman, juara dunia empat kali.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: A few have been in this position for Paraguay, but he scores! Paraguay have done it! Paraguay won it! MELAT KIROS (D),
paragraphs.cn: Tim Paraguay punya beberapa peluang, tapi dia mencetak gol! Paraguay berhasil! Paraguay menang! Merat Quiros (Demokrat):

paragraphs.speaker: Colorado Congressional Candidate
paragraphs.text: Experience only matters if you actually do something with it. And we're finding now with a lot of incumbent Democrats, that they're not actually able to deliver meaningfully on the things that working families need right now.
paragraphs.cn: Pengalaman hanya berarti jika diterapkan. Kami sekarang menemukan bahwa banyak anggota DPR Demokrat petahana sebenarnya tidak dapat memenuhi kebutuhan keluarga pekerja saat ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, it's primary day in Colorado. Democrats are eyeing one particular congressional district, which sees a 30-year-long incumbent, Diana Degette, fighting for her seat against 29-year-old lawyer and Democratic socialist Melat Kiros. Degette took over in office the year that Kiros was born. Democratic socialists have successfully advanced in primaries in New York City, Washington, D.C., and L.A. Now, establishment Democrats are wondering if this wave will crash into Colorado today. The group chat is back.
paragraphs.cn: Hari ini adalah hari pemilihan pendahuluan di Colorado. Demokrat fokus pada satu distrik Kongres tertentu: petahana yang telah menjabat selama 30 tahun, Diana DeGette, bersaing dengan pengacara berusia 29 tahun dan sosialis demokrat, Merat Quiros, untuk memperebutkan kursi. Tahun DeGette menjabat, Quiros baru lahir. Sosialis demokrat telah berhasil maju dalam pemilihan pendahuluan di New York City, Washington DC, dan Los Angeles. Sekarang, Demokrat mapan ingin tahu apakah gelombang ini akan melanda Colorado hari ini. Obrolan grup dimulai lagi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I want to start with my Democratic adviser here.
paragraphs.cn: Saya ingin mendengar pendapat konsultan Demokrat saya dulu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Why is Colorado close? I have to admit, I looked at Colorado as a place where the Democrats who appealed in purple states had sort of made a home and were showing they could do it. And yet even Michael Bennet, the senator, people are looking at his seat now and be like, well, wait a second, what's going on? So, why are they vulnerable there?
paragraphs.cn: Mengapa Colorado kompetitif? Saya harus akui, saya dulu mengira Colorado adalah tempat di mana Demokrat yang menarik di negara bagian swing bisa menetap dan membuktikan diri. Namun, bahkan Senator Michael Bennett, orang-orang sekarang melihat kursinya dan berpikir: tunggu, ada apa? Jadi, mengapa mereka rentan di sana?

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: People keep forgetting that Colorado is one of those states that 20 years ago was a purple state, almost a red state.
paragraphs.cn: Orang selalu lupa bahwa Colorado 20 tahun lalu adalah salah satu negara bagian ungu, hampir merah.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: And it's become more and more blue as you've had lots of migration into there. But what you're --
paragraphs.cn: Dengan banyaknya pendatang baru, warnanya semakin biru. Tapi yang Anda lihat -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: And it's been periwinkle.
paragraphs.cn: Dan itu selalu biru muda.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: But what you see there is you see this inside versus outside game.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi yang Anda lihat di sana adalah pertarungan internal vs eksternal.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: This is not much about Democratic socialists against conservatives or however the media or operatives like me want to categorize it. But it's about you see these incumbents, and here's the pattern that's emerged even from New York to Colorado, is you have lots of really safe Democrats I've talked about on this show. Lots of Democrats have very safe congressional seats, just like lots of Republicans do.
paragraphs.cn: Ini bukan tentang sosialis demokrat vs konservatif, atau seperti yang media atau operator seperti saya ingin kategorikan, melainkan, Anda melihat para petahana ini, dari New York hingga Colorado, muncul pola bahwa ada banyak Demokrat yang sangat aman, yang saya bicarakan di acara ini. Banyak Demokrat memiliki kursi Kongres yang sangat aman, seperti juga banyak Republikan.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: And what that causes you to do is not spend any money talking to voters over a long period of time, because you're safe cycle after cycle.
paragraphs.cn: Akibatnya, Anda tidak perlu mengeluarkan uang untuk berkomunikasi dengan pemilih dalam jangka panjang, karena Anda aman dari satu siklus ke siklus berikutnya.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: And when you have these insurgent candidates where there's money being spent in a time where incumbents are looked at as poison, Democrat or Republican.
paragraphs.cn: Dan ketika kandidat penantang ini muncul, saat petahana dianggap sebagai racun, baik Partai Demokrat maupun Republik, ada dana yang masuk.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: You see an insurgency ready to happen.
paragraphs.cn: Anda akan melihat pemberontakan akan terjadi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So "Politico" is writing about Degette, and one of these on background types said it's not looking great. And it's very tough when you're fighting against a wave. And getting to that word, you're talking about, the wave. I think the thing that I wrestle with is that there are so many, both progressives and even, let's say, Congressional Black Caucus has this problem.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi Politico melaporkan tentang DeGette, dan salah satu sumber anonim mengatakan situasinya tidak terlalu optimis. Saat Anda melawan gelombang, ini sangat sulit. Berbicara tentang kata itu, gelombang yang Anda sebutkan. Saya pikir yang saya permasalahkan adalah, terlalu banyak orang, baik progresif, atau bahkan, misalnya, Congressional Black Caucus juga punya masalah ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Latino Caucus has this problem. These guys have been in office for so long, but they only just now are starting to get gavels and be in power. And they're, like, trying to turn to the voter and be like, no, no, no, no, no, no. We just -- we're going to do it now.
paragraphs.cn: Hispanic Caucus juga punya masalah ini. Orang-orang ini sudah lama menjabat, tetapi baru sekarang mereka mulai mendapatkan kekuasaan dan posisi kepemimpinan. Mereka mencoba beralih ke pemilih dan berkata, tidak, tidak, tidak, tidak, tidak. Kami hanya -- kami akan melakukannya sekarang.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: It's important to say this. Just one second. Degette, who she's running against, is in the Progressive Caucus.
paragraphs.cn: Ini penting, tunggu sebentar. Lawan DeGette adalah anggota Progressive Caucus.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: She's a very progressive member.
paragraphs.cn: Dia adalah anggota parlemen yang sangat progresif.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: She just hasn't spent any money talking to voters in a long time.
paragraphs.cn: Dia hanya sudah lama tidak mengeluarkan uang untuk berkomunikasi dengan pemilih.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: She also has a really reasonable -- I mean, like I know from the Republican side, for her and Bennet, Republicans like to work with both of them. So that's a problem right there when they're in this election. But I agree with you. Everyone's too old, and everyone is just tired of the status quo.
paragraphs.cn: Dia juga memiliki alasan yang sangat masuk akal -- maksud saya, dari sisi Republik saya tahu, untuk dia dan Bennet, Republik suka bekerja sama dengan mereka berdua. Jadi dalam pemilu ini, itu sendiri adalah masalah. Tapi saya setuju dengan Anda. Semua orang terlalu tua, semua orang bosan dengan status quo.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. And I'm the first to be out here talking about gerontocracy. I'm not going to act as though I've been immune to this language, but I have wrestled with it as I've thought about, like, some of these -- again, the progressives, the black, Latino. There's -- there's some affinity groups in Congress where they really never got into positions of power until now, right? When people are like, well, wait a second. You're too old to do the job.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Dan saya adalah orang pertama yang angkat bicara tentang gerontokrasi. Saya tidak akan berpura-pura tidak terpengaruh oleh pernyataan ini, tetapi saya memang bergumul dengan pertanyaan-pertanyaan ini -- misalnya, para progresif, kulit hitam, Latin. Ada kelompok kepentingan di Kongres yang baru sekarang benar-benar mendapatkan posisi kekuasaan, kan? Ketika orang berkata, tunggu, Anda terlalu tua untuk pekerjaan ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Yes. The other -- the other factor looming over this is that you saw this with Republicans in 2012 and 2016, that when a party is out of power and sort of in the wilderness, people are even hungrier to sort of, as both of you were saying, to sort of upset the world and see a lot of the older --
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Masalah lain yang membayangi adalah, Anda melihatnya pada Partai Republik pada tahun 2012 dan 2016, ketika sebuah partai kehilangan kekuasaan dan berada di padang gurun, orang-orang bahkan lebih ingin -- seperti yang Anda berdua katakan -- menjungkirbalikkan dunia, melihat banyak yang lebih tua --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, because why not? You're already in the wilderness.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, kenapa tidak? Lagipula sudah di padang gurun.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: Why not? Why not, we're in the wilderness.
paragraphs.cn: Kenapa tidak? Kenapa tidak, kita di padang gurun.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: And -- and also we -- various factions have different visions for how the future ought to be. And they're very, very different. Again, you saw it with the Republicans. And that led, in some part, to the Tea Party, but also to Donald Trump.
paragraphs.cn: Dan -- dan kami -- faksi yang berbeda memiliki visi yang berbeda tentang bagaimana seharusnya masa depan. Mereka sangat, sangat berbeda. Sekali lagi, Anda melihat ini pada Partai Republik. Ini sebagian menyebabkan Tea Party, tetapi juga Donald Trump.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Can I talk about this split?
paragraphs.cn: Bisakah saya berbicara tentang perpecahan ini?

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: -- what the party was.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- apa yang dulu menjadi partai ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Let me talk about the split divisions for a minute, because where this surfaces over and over again is this counting of how many socialists may be coming. We are panicking about that. This is our manifesto. Senator Chris Coons was talking about this on Friday. Here was his take. And we hear this take often. SEN. CHRIS COONS (D-DE): I'm a member of the Democratic Party. I'm not a member of the Socialist Party.
paragraphs.cn: Biarkan saya berbicara tentang faksi yang terpecah, karena masalah ini muncul berulang kali, yaitu statistik tentang berapa banyak sosialis yang mungkin datang. Kami panik tentang ini. Ini adalah manifesto kami. Senator Chris Coons membicarakan hal ini pada hari Jumat. Berikut pendapatnya. Kami sering mendengar pendapat ini. Senator Chris Coons (D-DE): Saya anggota Partai Demokrat. Saya bukan anggota Partai Sosialis.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And frankly, I think that, while folks can take ideas from other political traditions, if you're running in a Democratic primary, you ought to be a Democrat.
paragraphs.cn: Terus terang, saya pikir, meskipun orang dapat mengambil ide dari tradisi politik lain, jika Anda mengikuti pemilihan pendahuluan Demokrat, Anda harus menjadi seorang Demokrat.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And then in the meantime, poor Hakeem Jeffries --
paragraphs.cn: Sementara itu, Hakeem Jeffries yang malang --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- has to be asked about this over and over again. He is, as we say, House minority leader, hoping to change that in November. And people are saying, are you worried about the fall? Here's what he had to say on Monday.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- harus terus-menerus ditanya pertanyaan ini. Seperti yang kami katakan, dia adalah Pemimpin Minoritas DPR, berharap mengubah ini pada bulan November. Orang-orang bertanya, apakah Anda khawatir tentang pemilu musim gugur? Berikut yang dia katakan pada hari Senin.

paragraphs.speaker: Manu Raju
paragraphs.text: Mr. Jeffries, do you think that the -- all these socialists who are coming in here, is that good for your party right now? REP. HAKEEM JEFFRIES (D-NY): The party is going to continue to focus on winning seats currently held by Republicans and flipping them blue.
paragraphs.cn: Tuan Jeffries, apakah menurut Anda semua sosialis yang berdatangan ke sini ini baik untuk partai Anda sekarang? Anggota DPR Hakeem Jeffries (D-NY): Partai kami akan terus fokus pada memenangkan kursi yang saat ini dipegang oleh Partai Republik dan mengubahnya menjadi biru.

paragraphs.speaker: Raju
paragraphs.text: Yes, but they're going to come in, and they're going to demand the moon, and you're not going to be able to deliver the moon.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, tapi setelah mereka datang, mereka akan meminta bulan di langit, dan Anda tidak bisa meraih bulan.

paragraphs.speaker: Jeffries
paragraphs.text: Well, let's cross that bridge when we get to it, because I think, before we can even get to governing, we have to win.
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, nanti ada jalan, karena saya pikir, sebelum memikirkan pemerintahan, kita harus memenangkan pemilu dulu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Pro tip: when they walk slow, they want to answer.
paragraphs.cn: Tips: Saat mereka berjalan sangat lambat, itu berarti mereka ingin menjawab pertanyaan.

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.text: This is part of, you know, this broader debate that's taking place in the Democratic Party right now, leading into not just the midterm elections. I know that you hate it when I talk about 2028, Audie.
paragraphs.cn: Ini adalah bagian dari perdebatan yang lebih luas yang sedang berlangsung di internal Partai Demokrat, tidak hanya menyangkut pemilu paruh waktu. Saya tahu Anda tidak suka saya membicarakan tahun 2028, Audi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: No, I'm there. I'm there. We're doing it. We're doing it as it comes.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, saya siap. Saya siap. Kami sedang melakukannya. Kami mengikuti arus.

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.text: OK. But leading into the 2028 presidential race is the -- the country determines what it wants after Donald Trump, who is term- limited and whether or not the Democratic Party, you know, fighting against Donald Trump is enough. Or if there has to be some other set of policies and ideas. And if so, what exactly those are. You see that playing out in New York, in Colorado, and across the country right now.
paragraphs.cn: Baik. Namun sebelum pemilihan presiden 2028, negara harus memutuskan apa yang diinginkan setelah Trump yang masa jabatannya terbatas, dan apakah perjuangan Partai Demokrat melawan Trump sudah cukup. Atau apakah diperlukan serangkaian kebijakan dan ideologi lain. Jika ya, apa sebenarnya itu. Anda sekarang bisa melihat situasi ini di New York, Colorado, dan di seluruh negeri.

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.text: But it's not just, by the way, on the Democratic side, although I know that's what we're talking about right now. And I think this is what you were about to get into --
paragraphs.cn: Tapi omong-omong, ini tidak hanya terjadi di pihak Demokrat, meskipun saya tahu kita sedang membahas ini. Dan saya pikir Anda akan membahas -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. We didn't even get into the Republican side of the Colorado race.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Kami bahkan belum membahas pihak Republik dalam pemilihan di Colorado.

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.text: I see this happening on the Republican side, as well, with the country. Republicans, at least in this country, repeatedly siding with President Donald Trump when he backs candidates over people who are establishment, if he simply says that this person wasn't supportive enough.
paragraphs.cn: Saya juga melihat hal yang sama terjadi di pihak Republik. Setidaknya di negara ini, para Republikan berulang kali berpihak pada Presiden Donald Trump ketika dia mendukung kandidat daripada petahana, selama dia mengatakan orang ini tidak cukup mendukungnya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Republicans in the primaries.
paragraphs.cn: Pemilihan pendahuluan Republik.

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.text: In the primaries. In the primaries.
paragraphs.cn: Dalam pemilihan pendahuluan. Dalam pemilihan pendahuluan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And for both parties, the question is the general.
paragraphs.cn: Bagi kedua partai, masalahnya adalah pemilihan umum.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: But it's also -- it's also important to note we're talking about blue district primaries in blue districts, in blue states. These are --
paragraphs.cn: Tapi yang juga penting, kita berbicara tentang daerah pemilihan biru, pemilihan pendahuluan di daerah pemilihan biru di negara bagian biru. Ini adalah -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: But let me push back.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi biarkan saya membantah.

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.text: -- you know, this is not the entire country.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- Anda tahu, ini bukan gambaran seluruh negara.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: Let me push back tonight. In -- in Colorado Eight tonight, in this race today that they're having in Colorado, it's a true marginal seat that we're talking about. It's one of those 40 seats that is literally 50/50. It's just North of downtown. My PAC, in full disclosure, has run some ads there for a Latino that's running, a young Latino who's 31, running against a sitting state senator there. She's 57. Between the two Democrats. Also, there's a freshman Republican.
paragraphs.cn: Biarkan saya membantah malam ini. Di distrik ke-8 Colorado malam ini, dalam pemilihan yang mereka lakukan hari ini, kita berbicara tentang kursi yang benar-benar tipis. Ini adalah salah satu dari 40 kursi yang benar-benar imbang. Tepat di utara pusat kota. Komite aksi politik saya, dengan transparan, memasang beberapa iklan di sana untuk seorang kandidat Latino yang mencalonkan diri, dia berusia 31 tahun, Latino muda, bersaing dengan senator negara bagian petahana. Dia berusia 57 tahun. Keduanya adalah Demokrat. Selain itu, ada juga seorang Republikan baru.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: This seat will determine who controls Congress and many seats just like that.
paragraphs.cn: Kursi ini akan menentukan siapa yang mengendalikan Kongres, dan banyak kursi seperti ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Who's going to win that one? The -- the --
paragraphs.cn: Siapa yang akan memenangkan kursi itu? -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: The young guy's probably going to win it.
paragraphs.cn: Pemuda itu kemungkinan besar akan menang.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, don't ask him, will you put money on the race? But also, sir, you're saying 51 is old?
paragraphs.cn: Baik, jangan tanya dia, apakah Anda akan bertaruh pada pertandingan ini? Tapi, Pak, apakah Anda mengatakan usia 51 itu tua?

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: I'm saying in this context it is.
paragraphs.cn: Maksud saya dalam kasus ini tua.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: It's a generational change.
paragraphs.cn: Ini adalah pergantian generasi.

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Rocha
paragraphs.text: Fifty-one is too old.
paragraphs.cn: 51 tahun terlalu tua.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Hope and change. Chuck Rocha, Elliot, thank you for being here. I love when you enter the chat. After the break on CNN THIS MORNING from breaking NFL records to fighting for his life. Chris Johnson reveals his life-changing diagnosis. Plus, a dangerous heat wave set up across the Eastern half of the U.S., taking you all the way into the July 4th holiday weekend. And Washington, D.C., expected to top out Saturday in the triple digits, forecasted to have its hottest Fourth of July on record.
paragraphs.cn: Harapan dan perubahan. Chuck Rocha, Elliot, terima kasih sudah datang. Saya suka Anda bergabung dalam diskusi. Setelah istirahat, acara CNN This Morning akan beralih dari memecahkan rekor NFL hingga berjuang untuk hidup. Chris Johnson mengungkapkan diagnosis yang mengubah hidupnya. Selain itu, gelombang panas berbahaya melanda separuh timur AS, berlangsung hingga akhir pekan liburan 4 Juli. Washington DC diperkirakan suhu akan mencapai tiga digit pada hari Sabtu, berpotensi menjadi 4 Juli terpanas yang pernah tercatat.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It's now 25 minutes past the hour. This is your morning roundup: some of the stories you might have missed. Did you know there are survivors still being found in Venezuela after last night's earthquakes? Crews rescued a 12-year-old who was trapped underneath the rubble. Now, this came hours after a 21-year-old man was pulled from underneath a building that collapsed. He'd been trapped for days. And here's what he had to say after the rescue. AARON LEVI CANTILLO, RESCUED MAN (through translator): I'm a nobody.
paragraphs.cn: Sekarang pukul 25 menit lewat. Ini adalah ringkasan berita pagi Anda: beberapa cerita yang mungkin Anda lewatkan. Tahukah Anda, tadi malam masih ada korban selamat yang ditemukan setelah gempa di Venezuela? Tim penyelamat menyelamatkan seorang anak berusia 12 tahun yang terperangkap di bawah reruntuhan. Dan beberapa jam sebelumnya, seorang pria berusia 21 tahun diselamatkan dari bawah bangunan yang runtuh. Dia terperangkap selama berhari-hari. Berikut adalah apa yang dia katakan setelah diselamatkan. Pria yang selamat, Aaron Levi Cantillo (melalui penerjemah): Saya bukan siapa-siapa.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I'm just a young man who spent five days trapped beneath a building. But I'm here today, and I'm alive, thanks to my God. He is the only one who got me out of there and sent his angels. Who were his angels? The people who worked tirelessly on my rescue. The people who cared about how I was doing, the people who were there when I came out, who shouted my name when they saw me emerge. As far as I know, I was just informed that I was the only person in that building who was found alive.
paragraphs.cn: Saya hanya seorang pemuda yang terperangkap di bawah bangunan selama lima hari. Tapi saya di sini hari ini, saya masih hidup, terima kasih kepada Tuhanku. Dialah satu-satunya yang mengeluarkan saya dari sana dan mengirim malaikat. Siapa malaikatnya? Mereka yang bekerja tanpa lelah untuk penyelamatan saya, mereka yang peduli dengan kondisi saya, mereka yang hadir saat saya keluar, mereka yang memanggil nama saya saat melihat saya muncul. Sejauh yang saya tahu, saya diberitahu bahwa saya adalah satu-satunya yang ditemukan hidup di gedung itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: In the meantime, there are crews finding pets barking from beneath the damage, which led them to pull this puppy to safety. Now, these are only some of the lucky few who have been found alive. The death toll currently stands at around 1,700; 5,000 people injured. Many more remain missing. Former NFL star running back Chris Johnson revealed in a TV interview that he suffers from ALS. The 40-year-old spent ten years in the NFL and last played in 2017.
paragraphs.cn: Sementara itu, tim penyelamat mendengar hewan peliharaan menggonggong di bawah reruntuhan, sehingga berhasil menyelamatkan anjing kecil ini. Ini hanyalah beberapa korban selamat yang beruntung. Saat ini jumlah korban tewas sekitar 1.700 orang, 5.000 orang terluka. Masih banyak lagi yang hilang. Mantan bintang NFL running back Chris Johnson mengungkapkan dalam wawancara televisi bahwa dia menderita Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS). Pemain berusia 40 tahun ini bermain di NFL selama sepuluh tahun, pertandingan terakhirnya pada tahun 2017.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, in his interview on ABC's "Good Morning America," he was only able to communicate through a computerized speech generation device.
paragraphs.cn: Dalam wawancara dengan ABC 'Good Morning America', dia hanya bisa berkomunikasi melalui perangkat penghasil suara komputer.

paragraphs.speaker: Chris Johnson
paragraphs.text: I first noticed weakness in my right hand. At first it was little things like my grip didn't feel right, and I wasn't as strong as I've always been. It's continued to progress much faster than I ever imagined.
paragraphs.cn: Awalnya saya perhatikan tangan kanan saya lemah. Awalnya hal-hal kecil, seperti kekuatan genggaman terasa tidak benar, tidak sekuat dulu. Tapi perkembangan penyakitnya jauh lebih cepat dari yang saya bayangkan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, Johnson is one of only nine players in NFL history to rush more than 2,000 yards in a season. And Paraguay shocked Germany in a massive upset last night on penalties.
paragraphs.cn: Saat ini, Johnson adalah salah satu dari sembilan pemain dalam sejarah NFL yang berlari lebih dari 2.000 yard dalam satu musim. Dan Paraguay mengejutkan dunia dengan mengalahkan Jerman dalam adu penalti tadi malam.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Down to Jose Canale. A few have been in this position for Paraguay, but he scores! Paraguay have done it! Paraguay have won it! And they've booted Germany out of the World Cup!
paragraphs.cn: Giliran José Canale. Beberapa pemain Paraguay sebelumnya pernah berdiri di posisi ini, tapi dia mencetak gol! Paraguay berhasil! Paraguay menang! Mereka mengeliminasi Jerman dan menendangnya keluar dari Piala Dunia!

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Woof. The four-time World Cup winners now out of this year's championships. It's the first time the Germans have ever lost in the World Cup on penalties. Paraguay advancing to the Round of 16 later this week. And straight ahead on CNN THIS MORNING, deleted tweets from a rising Democratic figure now raising questions about socialist views inside the party. Plus, what a key vote on military aid to Israel might say about deep divisions inside the party, as well. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: Wow. Juara Piala Dunia empat kali kini tersingkir dari turnamen ini. Ini adalah pertama kalinya Jerman kalah dalam adu penalti di Piala Dunia. Paraguay akan melaju ke babak 16 besar akhir pekan ini. Selanjutnya di CNN 'This Morning', tweet yang dihapus oleh seorang bintang baru Demokrat mempertanyakan pandangan sosialis di dalam partai. Selain itu, pemungutan suara penting untuk bantuan militer Israel juga mengungkap perpecahan mendalam di dalam partai.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: A CNN KFILE investigation uncovered deleted social media posts from Democratic congressional nominee Darializa Avila Chevalier's old Twitter account. So, the posts include favorable references to communism, Marxist ideology, and authoritarian communist figures. She says she has grown considerably since those posts. And when asked about President Trump accusing her of being a communist, she said this. DARIALIZA AVILA CHEVALIER (D),
paragraphs.cn: Investigasi CNN KFILE menemukan bahwa akun Twitter lama kandidat Kongres Demokrat Daria Liza Avila Chevalier menghapus postingan media sosial. Postingan tersebut berisi penyebutan positif terhadap ideologi komunis, Marxis, dan tokoh komunis otoriter. Dia mengatakan bahwa dia sudah banyak berkembang sejak postingan itu dipublikasikan. Ketika ditanya tentang Presiden Trump yang menuduhnya sebagai komunis, dia menjawab seperti ini. Daria Liza Avila Chevalier (Demokrat):

paragraphs.speaker: U.S. House Candidate From New York
paragraphs.text: You know, I think that is -- that framing is one that I've been very proud to be able to say I don't respond to. One in which I have been very intentional to say, I won't be reactive.
paragraphs.cn: Anda tahu, saya pikir kerangka ini adalah sesuatu yang saya bangga bisa mengatakan saya tidak menanggapinya. Saya sengaja mengatakan, saya tidak akan bereaksi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Group chat is back. I wanted to talk about this for two reasons. One, the tweets. This is a second round of going through her social media posts and finding politically unsavory things. Before we get to that, her saying, I'm just not going to react, is that going to work when you think of the consultants that are out there writing their ads right now, with just pictures of Marx and Lenin and a stamp over her face?
paragraphs.cn: Grup obrolan kembali lagi. Saya ingin membahas ini karena dua alasan. Pertama, tweet-tweet ini. Ini adalah putaran kedua penggalian postingan media sosialnya, menemukan konten yang tidak populer secara politik. Sebelumnya, dia berkata dia hanya tidak akan bereaksi, ketika Anda memikirkan para konsultan yang sedang membuat iklan, mereka sekarang menggunakan foto Marx dan Lenin, dan mencapnya di wajahnya, apakah itu berhasil?

paragraphs.speaker: Chuck Rocha
paragraphs.text: Two things can happen at the same time. She can regret those tweets. She can say that they were stupid. She can not respond to them, like she did. But I'll promise you, there is a Republican out there now taking those tweets and going to put them on an ad that says all Democrats are this way.
paragraphs.cn: Dua hal bisa terjadi bersamaan. Dia bisa menyesali tweet-tweet itu. Dia bisa mengatakan tweet-tweet itu bodoh. Dia bisa tidak menanggapinya seperti yang dia lakukan. Tapi saya jamin, sekarang ada seorang Republikan yang mengambil tweet-tweet itu, membuatnya menjadi iklan, dan mengatakan semua Demokrat seperti itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Ashley Davis
paragraphs.text: But for her seat, it doesn't matter. I mean she's going to win no matter what.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi untuk distriknya, itu tidak penting. Maksud saya, bagaimanapun dia akan menang.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: So, I would think, in her -- in her situation --
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, saya pikir, dalam kasusnya -- dalam kasusnya --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, here's just a sample. Here is President Trump, how he plans on talking about it.
paragraphs.cn: Nah, ini hanya contoh. Beginilah cara Presiden Trump berniat membicarakan hal ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Right, but for her situation --
paragraphs.cn: Ya, tapi dalam kasusnya --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: On Monday, this is how he talked about the whole socialism thing.
paragraphs.cn: Pada hari Senin, dia berbicara tentang seluruh masalah sosialisme seperti ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Yes. Not responding.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Tidak ada tanggapan.

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.text: It's not socialism. It's really communism. They use the word social Democrat because it sounds so nice, but it's really communism you're talking about. I think it's the biggest threat to our nation there is, maybe since our founding. That includes World War I, World War II, September 11th. It includes the Pearl Harbor attack.
paragraphs.cn: Ini bukan sosialisme. Ini sebenarnya komunisme. Mereka menggunakan istilah sosial demokrat karena kedengarannya bagus, tapi yang Anda bicarakan sebenarnya adalah komunisme. Saya pikir ini adalah ancaman terbesar bagi negara kita sejak berdirinya, termasuk Perang Dunia I, Perang Dunia II, 9/11, dan serangan Pearl Harbor.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, it's not whether or not she can -- this blue state or this blue district will vote for a person who's in their leaning. It's, when you paint the broad brush, how far does it reach and does it stick?
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, masalahnya bukan apakah dia bisa -- negara bagian biru atau distrik biru ini akan memilih seseorang yang cenderung ke arah mereka. Tapi, ketika Anda mencoret dengan satu goresan, seberapa jauh pengaruhnya bisa menjangkau, dan apakah itu bisa bertahan?

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Well, I think that -- and we were talking about this in the break -- that Donald Trump kind of normalized some of the things that you've said. And people seem to either want to care or not want to care. And they --
paragraphs.cn: Nah, saya pikir -- kami juga mendiskusikannya saat istirahat -- Donald Trump sampai batas tertentu membuat kata-kata yang Anda ucapkan menjadi normal. Orang-orang tampaknya peduli atau tidak peduli. Dan mereka --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: On social media specifically, yes.
paragraphs.cn: Terutama di media sosial, ya.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: On social media in general. But I have to say, she grew up, I would think, I don't know exactly how old she is, where --
paragraphs.cn: Secara umum di media sosial. Tapi saya harus katakan, dia -- saya pikir, saya tidak yakin berapa tepatnya usianya --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: She's young, yes, Through this whole social media world, where you just put your whole life out there.
paragraphs.cn: Dia masih muda, ya. Di dunia media sosial ini, Anda menaruh seluruh hidup Anda di sana.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: And so, I do think times are changing. But as I also was saying, like, in the business community, it's also changing. I threaten my son every day not to put things, which is probably why the disappearing apps are more popular than ever, because you can't go back and find those as easy.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, saya memang berpikir zaman berubah. Tapi seperti yang saya katakan, dunia bisnis juga berubah. Saya setiap hari mengancam anak saya untuk tidak menaruh barang sembarangan, itu mungkin sebabnya aplikasi yang menghilang setelah dibaca lebih populer dari sebelumnya, karena Anda tidak bisa dengan mudah mengambil kembali konten itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But you're saying we're at an age where it would be hard pressed to find someone in their 30s at this point --
paragraphs.cn: Tapi maksud Anda, kita sekarang di zaman di mana sulit menemukan seseorang berusia 30-an --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Correct. You live your life on this. I mean, yes.
paragraphs.cn: Tepat. Hidup Anda tergantung pada ini. Maksud saya, ya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Who doesn't have a social media background worth scrubbing. FRANCESCA CHAMBERS, WHITE HOUSE CORRESPONDENT, "USA TODAY": Why are you looking at me?
paragraphs.cn: Siapa yang tidak memiliki sejarah media sosial yang perlu dibersihkan? Koresponden Gedung Putih USA Today, Francesca Chambers: Kenapa kamu melihatku?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: You're a person who may.
paragraphs.cn: Kamu adalah seseorang yang mungkin memiliki (sejarah seperti itu).

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: You're the (INAUDIBLE) of us.
paragraphs.cn: Kamu adalah salah satu dari kami yang (tidak terdengar).

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I don't -- a young person. No, seriously, I wrestle with this because also it's -- I would think it's fair game. If Democrats can go scrubbing for old tweets where you find out there are Republican candidates who have said things that are racist, doesn't it -- isn't it fair game to go after somebody who has said, abolish police, or said things that actually are even directly related to policy?
paragraphs.cn: Aku bukan -- -- seorang anak muda. Tidak, serius, aku bergumul dengan ini karena ini juga -- -- aku pikir ini adalah permainan yang adil. Jika Demokrat bisa menggali tweet lama dan menemukan kandidat Republik mengatakan hal rasis, bukankah adil untuk mengejar orang yang mengatakan "defund the police" atau hal yang langsung terkait kebijakan?

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.text: I think regardless of what are in these social media posts from any candidate, that President Donald Trump and the Republican Party are going to make this argument about these Democratic socialists as alleged communists one way or another.
paragraphs.cn: Menurutku, apa pun konten media sosial kandidat, Presiden Donald Trump dan Partai Republik akan menyebut sosialis demokrat ini sebagai komunis dengan cara tertentu.

paragraphs.speaker: Chambers
paragraphs.text: I mean, he's made it, you know, very clear, going back to his comments on Friday, when he was at a religious conservative conference and he spent most of his speech, by the way, talking about this, talking about the threat of communism, explaining about how Democrats are going to try and offer you free stuff. And that might sound good for a couple of years.
paragraphs.cn: Maksudku, dia sudah sangat jelas, lihat kembali komentarnya hari Jumat, ketika dia berada di konferensi konservatif agama, omong-omong, sebagian besar pidatonya membahas ini, membahas ancaman komunisme, menjelaskan bagaimana Demokrat mencoba memberimu barang gratis. Ini mungkin terdengar bagus di beberapa tahun pertama.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Do you think it's weird they're skipping straight to communism? Like isn't -- I just want to show, positive opinions of socialism. In 2010, yes, there's movement from Democrats. Democrats now view socialism more favorably. But Americans in general don't view it all that much better than they did in 2010.
paragraphs.cn: Apa menurutmu aneh mereka langsung melompat ke komunisme? Misalnya, aku hanya ingin menunjukkan pandangan positif tentang sosialisme. Tahun 2010, ya, Demokrat berubah. Demokrat sekarang lebih positif tentang sosialisme. Tapi secara keseluruhan, pandangan orang Amerika tidak jauh lebih baik dari tahun 2010.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And I just wonder why you can't just critique socialism as is, and you have to go straight to communism.
paragraphs.cn: Aku hanya penasaran, kenapa kamu tidak bisa langsung mengkritik sosialisme itu sendiri, malah mengaitkannya dengan komunisme.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Communism is much more, if you're just branding them, and you know this as a communicator for campaigns, it's much easier to understand.
paragraphs.cn: Komunisme lebih ekstrem, jika kamu hanya memberi label, dan kamu tahu sebagai komunikator kampanye, ini lebih mudah dipahami.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: (INAUDIBLE) to go straight back to the '50s. (CROSS TALK)
paragraphs.cn: (Tidak terdengar) langsung kembali ke tahun 50-an. (Percakapan silang)

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Love it. OK. Well --
paragraphs.cn: Suka. Oke. Jadi -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: Absolutely. And what we come down to in this is, in a -- in a time in a midterm, for all of you at home, national polls and the way the sentiment doesn't matter. It only matters in a handful of congressional races, none of which that are in the inside of any major city. They're all in the exurbs or the suburbs. So, you're talking to a group -- different group of people than you would in a downtown urban setting.
paragraphs.cn: Tentu. Pada akhirnya, selama pemilu paruh waktu, bagi kalian di rumah, jajak pendapat nasional dan opini publik tidak penting. Yang penting hanyalah persaingan di beberapa distrik kongres, dan distrik-distrik itu tidak ada di pusat kota besar mana pun, semuanya di pinggiran kota atau pinggiran dekat. Jadi, kamu berbicara dengan sekelompok orang yang berbeda dari lingkungan pusat kota.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.text: And the real basis comes down to, do you care more about somebody's regretful tweet or the price of hamburger and eggs? That's what it's going to come down to.
paragraphs.cn: Masalah fundamental sebenarnya adalah, apakah kamu lebih peduli pada tweet seseorang yang memalukan, atau harga burger dan telur? Itulah intinya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, also, in a new media market. I'm going to turn to another topic before I go. Hasan Piker, the streamer, Democratic activist, has been backing a lot of Democratic socialist candidates. He said this about the idea of going into people's background and want to play it for you guys.
paragraphs.cn: Selain itu, di pasar media baru. Sebelum aku selesai, aku akan beralih ke topik lain. Pembawa acara, aktivis Demokrat Hasan Piker telah mendukung banyak kandidat sosialis demokrat. Dia berbicara tentang ide menggali latar belakang kandidat, aku ingin memutarkan untuk kalian.

paragraphs.speaker: Hasan Piker
paragraphs.text: If you are advocating or advancing society in a direction that capital owners don't want, they're going to come after you. They're going to come after you. And if they can't find anything in your background, they're going to invent stuff, right? We saw this with Nadya Alyssa (ph). They didn't have anything that they can hit her on, so they just started, you know, they just started creating controversy off of her tweets. This is how these guys operate.
paragraphs.cn: Jika kamu mengadvokasi atau mendorong masyarakat ke arah yang tidak diinginkan pemilik modal, mereka akan mencarimu. Mereka akan mencarimu. Jika mereka tidak menemukan apa pun di latar belakangmu, mereka akan mengarangnya, kan? Kita melihat ini pada Nadia Alisha (suara). Mereka tidak punya apa pun untuk menyerangnya, jadi mereka mulai, kamu tahu, mereka mulai menggunakan tweetnya untuk menciptakan kontroversi. Begitulah cara orang-orang ini beroperasi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: He was talking about oppositional research. He was -- you're nodding.
paragraphs.cn: Dia berbicara tentang penelitian lawan. Dia -- -- kamu mengangguk.

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: Yes, I mean, this is -- if you're going to run, everything is fair game. I mean this is why Chuck and I are never able to run for office.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, maksudku, jika kamu mencalonkan diri, semuanya adil. Itulah mengapa Chuck dan aku tidak akan pernah bisa mencalonkan diri untuk jabatan publik.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, let me state here --
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, biarkan aku menjelaskan di sini -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Davis
paragraphs.text: I mean this is like as old as time in regards to the -- everything's fair game. This is what you're putting yourself out there for.
paragraphs.cn: Maksudku, ini seperti pepatah lama -- -- semuanya adil. Itulah mengapa kamu menempatkan dirimu di garis depan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right. Well, another thing that's become fair game, and the reason why I wanted to play Piker is this conversation about Israel, which continues to divide the party, particularly the Democratic Party. Chevalier's opinions on that have also come up. Speaking on this sort of shifting landscape with Mayor Mamdani, who backed her, actually being asked to clarify his position on Israel. And here's what he had to say.
paragraphs.cn: Baik. Hal lain yang menjadi sorotan, dan juga alasan aku ingin memutar Piker, adalah tentang percakapan mengenai Israel, topik yang terus memecah belah partai, terutama Demokrat. Pandangan Chevalier tentang ini juga disebutkan. Berbicara tentang situasi yang terus berubah ini, walikota Mamdani yang mendukungnya sebenarnya diminta untuk mengklarifikasi posisinya terhadap Israel. Berikut yang dia katakan.

paragraphs.speaker: Jonathan Karl
paragraphs.text: And the idea of a Jewish state, Israel as a Jewish state, that's in the charter. That's the way it is now. I do you support that? MAYOR ZOHRAN MAMDANI (D),
paragraphs.cn: Dan konsep negara Yahudi, Israel sebagai negara Yahudi, ini tertulis dalam piagam. Sekarang seperti itu. Apakah kamu mendukung ini?

paragraphs.speaker: New York City
paragraphs.text: I've said time and again that I support the state of Israel as a state with equal rights. I believe that any state that --
paragraphs.cn: Aku telah berulang kali mengatakan, aku mendukung Israel sebagai negara dengan hak yang setara. Aku percaya negara mana pun -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Karl
paragraphs.text: But as a Jewish state is the question.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi pertanyaannya adalah sebagai negara Yahudi.

paragraphs.speaker: Mamdani
paragraphs.text: I think any state that privileges one religion over the other is one that I can't tell you I support, whether it be Israel or Saudi Arabia or anywhere else. And a lot of that comes back to a fundamental belief that we should all be considered equal, no matter what our faith is.
paragraphs.cn: Menurutku, negara mana pun yang mengistimewakan satu agama di atas agama lain, aku tidak bisa mengatakan aku mendukungnya, baik Israel, Arab Saudi, atau tempat lain. Ini sebagian besar berasal dari keyakinan dasar: apa pun keyakinan kita, kita harus diperlakukan setara.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, this week, House Democratic leaders opposed an amendment that would block $3.3 billion in military aid to Israel. Leadership calls the measure flawed. Then you've got this growing progressive base that is pushing to break ranks. And if this amendment makes it to the floor, it could turn into a defining litmus test for the American left. So, I want to talk to you guys for a couple reasons.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, minggu ini para pemimpin Partai Demokrat di DPR menentang amandemen yang akan memblokir bantuan militer sebesar $3,3 miliar ke Israel. Kepemimpinan mengatakan langkah itu cacat. Kemudian, basis progresif yang semakin kuat mendorong untuk melanggar aturan. Jika amandemen ini sampai pada pemungutan suara, itu bisa menjadi batu ujian yang menentukan bagi sayap kiri Amerika. Jadi, saya ingin berbicara dengan Anda, karena beberapa alasan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
